In (B) reduction of I2 occurs, so it will be an oxidising agent and H2O2 will be a reducing agent.

solution:
The pH range between 3.1 (red) and 4.4 (yellow) is the colour-change interval of methyl orange.

Weak base has pH > 7. So, when methyl orange indicator is added the solution turns yellow & if the solution is titrated against strong acid it turns pinkish red at the end point i.e., pH <3.1.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Disproportionation reactions are a special type of redox reactions. One of the reactants in a disproportionation reaction always contains an element that can exist in at least three oxidation states. The element of reacting species is in intermediate oxidation state and simultaneously gets oxidised and reduced. MnO42-,ClO2-,Cl2 and Mn3+ are having intermediate oxidation states.

Note: No option is correct in the actual question. One option is modified for its correctness.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
CoCl3.xNH3+AgNO3→AgCl↓2 mol

For 2 moles of AgCl precipitate out,only two chloride ions should be present out side the coordination sphere that is in the ionization sphere.

Hence, the formula of the complex should be CoNH35ClCl2

CoNH35ClCl2+AgNO3→AgCl↓2 mol

So, the value of x should be 5.

x=5.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
C6H5CWAOOH+NaOSBH→C6H5CSaltOONa+H2O

 Initially the conductance is low due to the feeble ionisation of acetic acid. On the addition of base, there is decrease in conductance(A→B) not only due to the replacement of H+ by Na+ but also suppresses the dissociation of benzoic acid due to common ion benzoate. But very soon, the conductance increases(B→C) on adding NaOH as NaOH neutralises the un-dissociated C6H5COOH to C6H5COONa which is the strong electrolyte. This increase in conductance continues raise up to the equivalence point. The graph near the equivalence point is curved due the hydrolysis of salt C6H5COONa. Beyond the equivalence point, conductance increases(C→D) more rapidly with the addition of NaOH due to the highly conducting OH− ions

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Potassium dichromate, K2Cr2O7, is a common inorganic chemical reagent, most commonly used as an oxidizing agent in various laboratory and industrial applications. 

The balanced reaction is,

Cr2O72-+14H++6e-→2Cr3++7H2O

X=14

Y=6

A=7

subject: Chemistry
solution:
An oxidising agent is a substance that oxidises other substances involved in the reaction by gaining or accepting electrons from them.

So the lowest oxidation state substance will go to the higher oxidation state, which means it reduces others. Hence, it acts as a reducing agent. -3 is the lowest oxidation state for nitrogen. Hence, it only acts as a reducing agent.

Hence option A is the answer.

Also, moles of Ca(OH)2=mm of H2SO4

12×20=10×M

Molarity of H2SO4=1M

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The given equation can be balanced using ion-electron method as shown below.

Fe2+→Fe3++e-         -oxidation Half reaction

6e-+14H++Cr2O72-→2Cr3++7H2O -Reduction Half reaction

The overall reaction is 

6Fe2++14H++Cr2O72-→6Fe3++2Cr3++7H2O

The values X=14,Y=2 and Z = 7

(X+ Y +Z)  = 14 + 2 +7

= 23

Potassium hydrogen phthalate is primary standard for NaOH. Formula for  Potassium hydrogen phthalate is C6H5O4K. It behaves as a monobasic acid.

Phenolphthalein is used as an indicator in this titration.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Disproportionation reaction, is a type of redox reaction involving simultaneous reduction and oxidation of atoms of the same element from one oxidation state (OS) to two different oxidation states.

2H2O2-1⟶2H2O-2+O20 : Disproportionation

2NO2+H2O→HNO3+HNO2 : Disproportionation

MnO4-+4H++3e-→MnO2+2H2O : reduction only

3MnO42-+4H+→2MnO4-+MnO2+4+2H2O : Disproportionation

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Mohr's salt ($FeSO_4 \cdot (NH_4)_2SO_4 \cdot 6H_2O$) is a source of $Fe^{2+}$ ions.
The redox reaction with potassium dichromate in acidic medium is:
$6Fe^{2+} + Cr_2O_7^{2-} + 14H^+ \to 6Fe^{3+} + 2Cr^{3+} + 7H_2O$.
Moles of $Fe^{2+}$ from Mohr's salt solution:
$n(Fe^{2+}) = 0.6 \text{ M} \times 0.750 \text{ L} = 0.45 \text{ mol}$.
From the stoichiometry, 6 moles of $Fe^{2+}$ require 1 mole of $Cr_2O_7^{2-}$.
Moles of $Cr_2O_7^{2-}$ required:
$n(Cr_2O_7^{2-}) = \frac{0.45}{6} = 0.075 \text{ mol}$.
Molarity of potassium dichromate:
$M = \frac{0.075 \text{ mol}}{0.200 \text{ L}} = 0.375 \text{ M} = 375 \times 10^{-3} \text{ M}$.
Therefore, $x = 375$.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
(A) $\mathrm{CH}_4(\mathrm{~g})+2 \mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \xrightarrow{\Delta} 2 \mathrm{CO}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l}) \Rightarrow$ combination reaction
(B) 2 NaH (s) $\xrightarrow{\Delta} 2 \mathrm{Na}(\mathrm{s}) \quad+\mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \quad \Rightarrow$ Decomposition reaction
(C) $\mathrm{V}_2 \mathrm{O}_5(\mathrm{~s})+5 \mathrm{Ca}(\mathrm{s}) \xrightarrow{\Delta} 2 \mathrm{~V}(\mathrm{~s})+5 \mathrm{CaO}(\mathrm{s})$ $\Rightarrow$ Displacement reaction
(D) $2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2$ (aq.) $\xrightarrow{\Delta} 2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})+\mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g})$ $\Rightarrow$ Disproportionation reaction
